title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 -  17 -  2017: Roman cisterns for region 5  in Google Earth

enrichment.summary: Kyle records an early morning vlog discussing audio/microphone problems with his setup, then describes his ongoing Google Earth project building a detailed 3D model of a Roman city (region 5), including cisterns, apartments, baths, shrines, and other structures. He explains his batch-building workflow, a frustrating bug where buildings scattered when he tried to recolor them, and his goal to finish the model by end of winter.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: His $275 microphone isn't working well and he needs a better solution, likely tied to getting a new Apple cell phone
Building a navigable ground-level model is valuable even if not archaeologically precise, because it gives a functional sense of scale and layout
Prefers doing all instances of one task at once before moving to the next, finds it more efficient

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My computer is making a loud sound because it's trying to cool down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a big fan under my computer that goes on all the time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The last video I uploaded had audio issues because the microphone wasn't helping.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't talk very loud, but my mom always has to tell me to be quiet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to know how to get better sound for my videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a $275 microphone, but it doesn't work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My cell phone can't transfer files to the computer, so I need a new one.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to get an Apple cell phone that's compatible for recording.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to do fancy videos standing in front of a green screen.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I give tours of buildings and mansions on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: You can build mansions on Google Earth and see views from there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ride a bicycle at 21 miles per hour in my Google Earth tours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been working on the Google Earth team world, specifically section region five.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to place 180 houses, 3700 apartment buildings, 75 baths, 15 shrines, and 22 grand egos in my Google Earth project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Building all these structures takes a lot of time and effort.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My computer started acting weird while I was working on the project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I build systems in stages, similar to how I work on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I traced out the different buildings and apartments in the system.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I color-coded the buildings, but some scattered when I tried to change their colors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I saved my file and restarted the computer when it started acting weird.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to finish section five to have a better understanding of what to do next.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I'll be done with the building project by the end of winter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worry about the project more than I should.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are different ways to model buildings on Google Earth, but my method allows for ground-level exploration.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The realm I'm creating on Google Earth is close enough to the actual realm for practical understanding.
